# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

stages: 
 - test   # for testing only, not used here as it's a requirement but included to maintain consistency with other requirements below   
- name: build_and_test    
  trigger: [merge_request]     
  rules:       
       - if $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request"   # only run on merge request events, not directly from the UI. This is to prevent CICD security issues   
         when ^(feature|release)     # match any MR title that starts with 'features' or 'releases', as per requirement 
           then allow manual      # allows triggering by hand (manually running `git push`, etc.)  
       - allow all        # default to allowing everything else. This is a catch-all for other scenarios not covered above   
     when: always         # run at the end of each job in case there are any failures 
     
variables:         
 DB_HOSTNAME : $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH   # Default branch name, set by GitLab CI/CD. This is used to tag deployments   
 DEPLOYMENTS_PATH : /var/www/html     # Path where the deployment files will be placed  (set in .gitlab-ci.yml)     
 RELEASE_NOTES: false   # Set this variable as true for automatic release notes generation, set to `false` by default   
 RUNNING_ENV : $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE     # Source of the pipeline (merge request or direct commit), used in deployment tags.  This is not a real environment but GitLab's way of distinguishing between different environments
